title: Pediatric Emergency Bed Assignment
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: pediatric emergency bed assignment.
assumptions: Patient cases (T1) represent discrete pediatric ED visits and include triage subgroup flags (T17/T18).
Resource pools (beds, devices, staff slots) are accurately reflected by initial availability facts.
Care protocols (T10) encode compatibility constraints between patient attributes and resource types.
Actions are atomic: reservation and release operations enforce exclusive access to limited resources.
Final assignment requires passing a set of readiness checks (staff, device, protocol compatibility).
risks: Deadlock risk if multiple encounters reserve disjoint required resources and cannot progress to completion, blocking throughput.
Mis-triage leading to inappropriate protocol binding and unsafe resource assignments for high-acuity patients.
Resource starvation where repeated provisional holds prevent definitive assignment for other patients.
Incorrect initial availability facts (missing or stale) causing allocation failures or unsafe assignments.
Over-reliance on single specialist slots or diagnostic resources that create single points of failure for assignment.
